title: The U.S. Hit 2,104 Measles Cases. Second Year in a Row.
script: Didn't we already fix this? The CDC confirmed this week that the United States has recorded 2,104 measles cases in 2026 — the second straight year the country has surpassed 2,000. Cases spread across 38 states. 93% are outbreak-associated. 92% of those infected were unvaccinated or had unknown vaccination status. Health officials are now openly questioning whether the U.S. can hold onto its measles-eliminated status, a title it earned in 2000 after decades of vaccination campaigns. That status is under formal review. The MMR vaccine is 97% effective against measles. But as vaccine hesitancy grows, so do the outbreaks. The CDC is warning that summer travel will make things worse. So: how many years in a row before the U.S. loses eliminated status?
